You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.174 (commit: b6ed71fd).

## Issue to Resolve

Feature Request: Implement 'User Action' Functionality Similar to AnkiMobile
In AnkiMobile (starting from version 2.0.88+), there is a feature that allows users to trigger JavaScript in the review screen. This advanced functionality is especially beneficial for those using shared decks that provide keyboard shortcuts to perform actions, such as revealing a hint or the next cloze deletion. By defining a user action, it's possible to trigger these actions with external devices, like a gamepad, or even by using gestures on the screen.

For instance, in AnkiMobile, if a card template provides a revealNext() function, users can instruct AnkiMobile to call it with the following code:

```
var userJs1 = revealNext;
```

This feature is especially handy for those who have written their own actions or are using certain shared decks/templates that provide these actions.

For more details, please refer to the [AnkiMobile documentation](https://docs.ankimobile.net/study-tools.html). Once on the page, scroll down to the "User Action 1-8" section for specifics.

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Hello! üëã Thanks for logging this issue. Please remember we are all volunteers here, so some patience may be required before we can get to the issue. Also remember that the fastest way to get resolution on an issue is to propose a change directly, https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/Contributing

Implementation should be similar to #10462 
Hello üëã, this issue has been opened for more than 3 months with no activity on it. If the issue is still here, please keep in mind that we need community support and help to fix it! Just comment something like _still searching for solutions_ and if you found one, please open a pull request! You have 7 days until this gets closed automatically
Any idea when this feature will be incorporated? Will it make to 2.17?
Depends on whether anyone submits a PR. Fancy giving it a shot?

Personally: working on getting 2.17 stable, won't have much capacity for enhancements until 2.18
I don't know anything about coding. If i did, i would have had loved to work on ankidroid. 
@salmanuc then you will just have to wait patiently, accept that there is no progress unless someone comments that they are working on it (so asking will not be helpful, sorry) and also accept that it may simply close out unimplemented. That's the way open source / volunteer projects work
I'd be happy to fund this if there's someone interested in doing it 
I can do that by the mid of the month
Hey @AnKingMed @BrayanDSO, wanted to ask if there was an update to this?
Specifically for AnkiDroid

Thanks for all that you do!
I forgot this issue. I'll take a look into it in the next couple of weeks
> I forgot this issue. I'll take a look into it in the next couple of weeks

Thanks alright, would be a timesaver üòÑ 
Hey @BrayanDSO
Wanted to ask if there was an update to this?
Specifically related to AnkiDroid

Thank you so much!
Had some personal issues, but I'll start this weekend
Hope you're doing well, and sorry for being annoying 
Thanks so much again
Do you, or anyone else, have a sample deck that uses it to share?

If it has any sensitive content and you don't want to share it publicly, you can send it to my email brayandso.dev@gmail.com
I've created a sample deck that has a few cards that use the one-by-one cloze reveal

Hope this works; if it doesn't, I'll be happy to help out in any way I can 

Here's the deck:
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1RqYEjuDi6WK2A0GKMHbzT8_TwaEDKYxm/view?usp=drivesdk

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/assets/scripts/card.js`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/Reviewer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/analytics/UsageAnalytics.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/ViewerCommand.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/10-preferences.xml`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/preferences.xml`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/xml/preferences_controls.xml`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 16‚Üípackage com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer
 17‚Üí
 18‚Üíimport android.content.SharedPreferences
 19‚Üíimport android.view.KeyEvent
 20‚Üíimport androidx.core.content.edit
 21‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.R
 22‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding.Companion.keyCode
 23‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding.Companion.unicode
 24‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding.ModifierKeys
 25‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding.ModifierKeys.Companion.ctrl
 26‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding.ModifierKeys.Companion.shift
 27‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.CardSide
 28‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding
 29‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.*
 30‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.Companion.fromPreference
 31‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.Companion.toPreferenceString
 32‚Üíimport java.util.*
 33‚Üíimport java.util.function.BiFunction
 34‚Üíimport java.util.stream.Collectors
 35‚Üí
 36‚Üí/** Abstraction: Discuss moving many of these to 'Reviewer'  */
 37‚Üíenum class ViewerCommand(val resourceId: Int) {
 38‚Üí    SHOW_ANSWER(R.string.show_answer),
 39‚Üí    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1(R.string.answer_again),
 40‚Üí    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2(R.string.answer_hard),
 41‚Üí    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3(R.string.answer_good),
 42‚Üí    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4(R.string.answer_easy),
 43‚Üí    UNDO(R.string.undo),
 44‚Üí    REDO(R.string.redo),
 45‚Üí    EDIT(R.string.cardeditor_title_edit_card),
 46‚Üí    MARK(R.string.menu_mark_note),
 47‚Üí    BURY_CARD(R.string.menu_bury_card),
 48‚Üí    SUSPEND_CARD(R.string.menu_suspend_card),
 49‚Üí    DELETE(R.string.menu_delete_note),
 50‚Üí    PLAY_MEDIA(R.string.gesture_play),
 51‚Üí    EXIT(R.string.gesture_abort_learning),
 52‚Üí    BURY_NOTE(R.string.menu_bury_note),
 53‚Üí    SUSPEND_NOTE(R.string.menu_suspend_note),
 54‚Üí    TOGGLE_FLAG_RED(R.string.gesture_flag_red),
 55‚Üí    TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE(R.string.gesture_flag_orange),
 56‚Üí    TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN(R.string.gesture_flag_green),
 57‚Üí    TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE(R.string.gesture_flag_blue),
 58‚Üí    TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK(R.string.gesture_flag_pink),
 59‚Üí    TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE(R.string.gesture_flag_turquoise),
 60‚Üí    TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE(R.string.gesture_flag_purple),
 61‚Üí    UNSET_FLAG(R.string.gesture_flag_remove),
 62‚Üí    PAGE_UP(R.string.gesture_page_up),
 63‚Üí    PAGE_DOWN(R.string.gesture_page_down),
 64‚Üí    TAG(R.string.add_tag),
 65‚Üí    CARD_INFO(R.string.card_info_title),
 66‚Üí    ABORT_AND_SYNC(R.string.gesture_abort_sync),
 67‚Üí    RECORD_VOICE(R.string.record_voice),
 68‚Üí    SAVE_VOICE(R.string.save_voice),
 69‚Üí    REPLAY_VOICE(R.string.replay_voice),
 70‚Üí    TOGGLE_WHITEBOARD(R.string.gesture_toggle_whiteboard),
 71‚Üí    CLEAR_WHITEBOARD(R.string.clear_whiteboard),
 72‚Üí    CHANGE_WHITEBOARD_PEN_COLOR(R.string.title_whiteboard_editor),
 73‚Üí    SHOW_HINT(R.string.gesture_show_hint),
 74‚Üí    SHOW_ALL_HINTS(R.string.gesture_show_all_hints),
 75‚Üí    ADD_NOTE(R.string.menu_add_note),
 76‚Üí    RESCHEDULE_NOTE(R.string.card_editor_reschedule_card);
 77‚Üí
 78‚Üí    companion object {
 79‚Üí        val allDefaultBindings: List<MappableBinding>
 80‚Üí            get() = Arrays.stream(entries.toTypedArray())
 81‚Üí                .flatMap { x: ViewerCommand -> x.defaultValue.stream() }
 82‚Üí                .collect(Collectors.toList())
 83‚Üí
 84‚Üí        fun fromPreferenceKey(key: String) = ViewerCommand.entries.first { it.preferenceKey == key }
 85‚Üí    }
 86‚Üí
 87‚Üí    val preferenceKey: String
 88‚Üí        get() = "binding_$name"
 89‚Üí
 90‚Üí    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 91‚Üí        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 92‚Üí            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
 93‚Üí            collection.remove(element)
 94‚Üí            collection.add(0, element)
 95‚Üí            true
 96‚Üí        }
 97‚Üí        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
 98‚Üí    }
 99‚Üí
100‚Üí    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
101‚Üí        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
102‚Üí            // do not reorder the elements
103‚Üí            if (collection.contains(element)) {
104‚Üí                return@BiFunction false
105‚Üí            }
106‚Üí            collection.add(element)
107‚Üí            return@BiFunction true
108‚Üí        }
109‚Üí        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
110‚Üí    }
111‚Üí
112‚Üí    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
113‚Üí        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
114‚Üí        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
115‚Üí        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
116‚Üí        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
117‚Üí    }
118‚Üí
119‚Üí    fun removeBinding(prefs: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
120‚Üí        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
121‚Üí        bindings.remove(binding)
122‚Üí        prefs.edit {
123‚Üí            putString(preferenceKey, bindings.toPreferenceString())
124‚Üí        }
125‚Üí    }
126‚Üí
127‚Üí    // If we use the serialised format, then this adds additional coupling to the properties.
128‚Üí    val defaultValue: List<MappableBinding>
129‚Üí        get() {
130‚Üí            // all of the default commands are currently for the Reviewer
131‚Üí            fun keyCode(keycode: Int, side: CardSide, modifierKeys: ModifierKeys = ModifierKeys.none()) =
132‚Üí                keyCode(keycode, Screen.Reviewer(side), modifierKeys)
133‚Üí            fun unicode(c: Char, side: CardSide) = unicode(c, Screen.Reviewer(side))
134‚Üí            return when (this) {
135‚Üí                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1 -> listOf(
136‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_Y, CardSide.BOTH),
137‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.ANSWER),
138‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.ANSWER)
139‚Üí                )
140‚Üí                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2 -> listOf(
141‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_X, CardSide.BOTH),
142‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.ANSWER),
143‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.ANSWER)
144‚Üí                )
145‚Üí                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3 -> listOf(
146‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_B, CardSide.BOTH),
147‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
148‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
149‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_DPAD_CENTER, CardSide.BOTH),
150‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_SPACE, CardSide.ANSWER),
151‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER),
152‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER)
153‚Üí                )
154‚Üí                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4 -> listOf(
155‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_A, CardSide.BOTH),
156‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.ANSWER),
157‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.ANSWER)
158‚Üí                )

1‚Üí/****************************************************************************************
  2‚Üí * Copyright (c) 2018 Mike Hardy <mike@mikehardy.net>                                   *
  3‚Üí *                                                                                      *
  4‚Üí * This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it under        *
  5‚Üí * the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by the Free Software        *
  6‚Üí * Foundation; either version 3 of the License, or (at your option) any later           *
  7‚Üí * version.                                                                             *
  8‚Üí *                                                                                      *
  9‚Üí * This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but WITHOUT ANY      *
 10‚Üí * WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A      *
 11‚Üí * PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU General Public License for more details.             *
 12‚Üí *                                                                                      *
 13‚Üí * You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along with         *
 14‚Üí * this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.                           *
 15‚Üí ****************************************************************************************/
 16‚Üí
 17‚Üípackage com.ichi2.anki.analytics
 18‚Üí
 19‚Üíimport android.content.Context
 20‚Üíimport android.content.SharedPreferences
 21‚Üíimport android.os.Build
 22‚Üíimport android.webkit.WebSettings
 23‚Üíimport androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 24‚Üíimport androidx.core.content.edit
 25‚Üíimport com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.GoogleAnalytics
 26‚Üíimport com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.GoogleAnalyticsConfig
 27‚Üíimport com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.httpclient.OkHttpClientImpl
 28‚Üíimport com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.request.DefaultRequest
 29‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidApp
 30‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.BuildConfig
 31‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.R
 32‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 33‚Üíimport com.ichi2.utils.DisplayUtils
 34‚Üíimport com.ichi2.utils.KotlinCleanup
 35‚Üíimport com.ichi2.utils.WebViewDebugging.hasSetDataDirectory
 36‚Üíimport org.acra.ACRA
 37‚Üíimport org.acra.util.Installation
 38‚Üíimport timber.log.Timber
 39‚Üí
 40‚Üí@KotlinCleanup("see if we can make variables lazy, or properties without the `s` prefix")
 41‚Üíobject UsageAnalytics {
 42‚Üí    const val ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY = "analytics_opt_in"
 43‚Üí
 44‚Üí    @KotlinCleanup("lateinit")
 45‚Üí    private var sAnalytics: GoogleAnalytics? = null
 46‚Üí    private var sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler: Thread.UncaughtExceptionHandler? = null
 47‚Üí    private var sOptIn = false
 48‚Üí    private var sAnalyticsTrackingId: String? = null
 49‚Üí    private var sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = -1
 50‚Üí
 51‚Üí    /**
 52‚Üí     * Initialize the analytics provider - must be called prior to sending anything.
 53‚Üí     * Usage after that is static
 54‚Üí     * Note: may need to implement sampling strategy internally to limit hits, or not track Reviewer...
 55‚Üí     *
 56‚Üí     * @param context required to look up the analytics codes for the app
 57‚Üí     */
 58‚Üí    @Synchronized
 59‚Üí    fun initialize(context: Context): GoogleAnalytics? {
 60‚Üí        Timber.i("initialize()")
 61‚Üí        if (sAnalytics == null) {
 62‚Üí            Timber.d("App tracking id 'tid' = %s", getAnalyticsTag(context))
 63‚Üí            val gaConfig = GoogleAnalyticsConfig()
 64‚Üí                .setBatchingEnabled(true)
 65‚Üí                .setSamplePercentage(getAnalyticsSamplePercentage(context))
 66‚Üí                .setBatchSize(1) // until this handles application termination we will lose hits if batch>1
 67‚Üí            sAnalytics = GoogleAnalytics.builder()
 68‚Üí                .withTrackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 69‚Üí                .withConfig(gaConfig)
 70‚Üí                .withDefaultRequest(
 71‚Üí                    AndroidDefaultRequest()
 72‚Üí                        .setAndroidRequestParameters(context)
 73‚Üí                        .applicationName(context.getString(R.string.app_name))
 74‚Üí                        .applicationVersion(BuildConfig.VERSION_CODE.toString())
 75‚Üí                        .applicationId(BuildConfig.APPLICATION_ID)
 76‚Üí                        .trackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 77‚Üí                        .clientId(Installation.id(context))
 78‚Üí                        .anonymizeIp(context.resources.getBoolean(R.bool.ga_anonymizeIp))
 79‚Üí                )
 80‚Üí                .withHttpClient(OkHttpClientImpl(gaConfig))
 81‚Üí                .build()
 82‚Üí        }
 83‚Üí        installDefaultExceptionHandler()
 84‚Üí        val userPrefs = context.sharedPrefs()
 85‚Üí        optIn = userPrefs.getBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, false)
 86‚Üí        userPrefs.registerOnSharedPreferenceChangeListener { sharedPreferences: SharedPreferences, key: String? ->
 87‚Üí            if (key == ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY) {
 88‚Üí                val newValue = sharedPreferences.getBoolean(key, false)
 89‚Üí                Timber.i("Setting analytics opt-in to: %b", newValue)
 90‚Üí                optIn = newValue
 91‚Üí            }
 92‚Üí        }
 93‚Üí        return sAnalytics
 94‚Üí    }
 95‚Üí
 96‚Üí    private fun getAnalyticsTag(context: Context): String? {
 97‚Üí        if (sAnalyticsTrackingId == null) {
 98‚Üí            sAnalyticsTrackingId = context.getString(R.string.ga_trackingId)
 99‚Üí        }
100‚Üí        return sAnalyticsTrackingId
101‚Üí    }
102‚Üí
103‚Üí    private fun getAnalyticsSamplePercentage(context: Context): Int {
104‚Üí        if (sAnalyticsSamplePercentage == -1) {
105‚Üí            sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = context.resources.getInteger(R.integer.ga_sampleFrequency)
106‚Üí        }
107‚Üí        return sAnalyticsSamplePercentage
108‚Üí    }
109‚Üí
110‚Üí    fun setDevMode() {
111‚Üí        Timber.d("setDevMode() re-configuring for development analytics tagging")
112‚Üí        sAnalyticsTrackingId = "UA-125800786-2"
113‚Üí        sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = 100
114‚Üí        reInitialize()
115‚Üí    }
116‚Üí
117‚Üí    /**
118‚Üí     * We want to send an analytics hit on any exception, then chain to other handlers (e.g., ACRA)
119‚Üí     */
120‚Üí    @Synchronized
121‚Üí    private fun installDefaultExceptionHandler() {
122‚Üí        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = Thread.getDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler()
123‚Üí        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler { thread: Thread?, throwable: Throwable ->
124‚Üí            sendAnalyticsException(throwable, true)
125‚Üí            if (thread == null) {
126‚Üí                Timber.w("unexpected: thread was null")
127‚Üí                return@setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler
128‚Üí            }
129‚Üí            sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler!!.uncaughtException(thread, throwable)
130‚Üí        }
131‚Üí    }
132‚Üí
133‚Üí    /**
134‚Üí     * Reset the default exception handler
135‚Üí     */
136‚Üí    @Synchronized
137‚Üí    private fun unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler() {
138‚Üí        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler(sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler)
139‚Üí        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = null
140‚Üí    }
141‚Üí
142‚Üí    /**
143‚Üí     * Allow users to enable or disable analytics
144‚Üí     */
145‚Üí    @set:Synchronized
146‚Üí    private var optIn: Boolean
147‚Üí        get() {
148‚Üí            Timber.d("getOptIn() status: %s", sOptIn)
149‚Üí            return sOptIn
150‚Üí        }
151‚Üí        private set(optIn) {
152‚Üí            Timber.i("setOptIn(): from %s to %s", sOptIn, optIn)
153‚Üí            sOptIn = optIn
154‚Üí            sAnalytics!!.flush()
155‚Üí            sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled = optIn
156‚Üí            sAnalytics!!.performSamplingElection()
157‚Üí            Timber.d("setOptIn() optIn / sAnalytics.config().enabled(): %s/%s", sOptIn, sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled)
158‚Üí        }
159‚Üí
160‚Üí    /**
161‚Üí     * Set the analytics up to log things, goes to hit validator. Experimental.
162‚Üí     *
163‚Üí     * @param dryRun set to true if you want to log analytics hit but not dispatch
164‚Üí     */
165‚Üí    @Synchronized
166‚Üí    fun setDryRun(dryRun: Boolean) {
167‚Üí        Timber.i("setDryRun(): %s, warning dryRun is experimental", dryRun)
168‚Üí    }
169‚Üí
170‚Üí    /**
171‚Üí     * Re-Initialize the analytics provider
172‚Üí     */
173‚Üí    @Synchronized
174‚Üí    fun reInitialize() {
175‚Üí        // send any pending async hits, re-chain default exception handlers and re-init
176‚Üí        Timber.i("reInitialize()")
177‚Üí        sAnalytics!!.flush()
178‚Üí        sAnalytics = null
179‚Üí        unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler()
180‚Üí        initialize(AnkiDroidApp.instance.applicationContext)
181‚Üí    }
182‚Üí
183‚Üí    /**
184‚Üí     * Submit a screen for aggregation / analysis.
185‚Üí     * Intended for use to determine if / how features are being used
186‚Üí     *
187‚Üí     * @param object the result of Object.getClass().getSimpleName() will be used as the screen tag
188‚Üí     */
189‚Üí    @KotlinCleanup("rename object")
190‚Üí    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(`object`: Any) {
191‚Üí        sendAnalyticsScreenView(`object`.javaClass.simpleName)
192‚Üí    }
193‚Üí
194‚Üí    /**
195‚Üí     * Submit a screen display with a synthetic name for aggregation / analysis
196‚Üí     * Intended for use if your class handles multiple screens you want to track separately
197‚Üí     *
198‚Üí     * @param screenName screenName the name to show in analysis reports
199‚Üí     */
200‚Üí    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(screenName: String) {
201‚Üí        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsScreenView(): %s", screenName)
202‚Üí        if (!optIn) {
203‚Üí            return
204‚Üí        }
205‚Üí        sAnalytics!!.screenView().screenName(screenName).sendAsync()
206‚Üí    }
207‚Üí
208‚Üí    /**
209‚Üí     * Send a detailed arbitrary analytics event, with noun/verb pairs and extra data if needed
210‚Üí     *
211‚Üí     * @param category the category of event, make your own but use a constant so reporting is good
212‚Üí     * @param action   the action the user performed
213‚Üí     * @param value    A value for the event, Integer.MIN_VALUE signifies caller shouldn't send the value
214‚Üí     * @param label    A label for the event, may be null
215‚Üí     */
216‚Üí    fun sendAnalyticsEvent(category: String, action: String, value: Int? = null, label: String? = null) {
217‚Üí        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsEvent() category/action/value/label: %s/%s/%s/%s", category, action, value, label)
218‚Üí        if (!optIn) {
219‚Üí            return
220‚Üí        }
221‚Üí        val event = sAnalytics!!.event().eventCategory(category).eventAction(action)
222‚Üí        if (label != null) {
223‚Üí            event.eventLabel(label)
224‚Üí        }
225‚Üí        if (value != null) {
226‚Üí            event.eventValue(value)
227‚Üí        }
228‚Üí        event.sendAsync()
229‚Üí    }
230‚Üí
231‚Üí    /**
232‚Üí     * Send an exception event out for aggregation/analysis, parsed from the exception information
233‚Üí     *
234‚Üí     * @param t     Throwable to send for analysis
235‚Üí     * @param fatal whether it was fatal or not
236‚Üí     */
237‚Üí    fun sendAnalyticsException(t: Throwable, fatal: Boolean) {
238‚Üí        sendAnalyticsException(getCause(t).toString(), fatal)
239‚Üí    }
240‚Üí
241‚Üí    @KotlinCleanup("convert to sequence")
242‚Üí    fun getCause(t: Throwable): Throwable {
243‚Üí        var cause: Throwable?
244‚Üí        var result = t
245‚Üí        while (null != result.cause.also { cause = it } && result != cause) {
246‚Üí            result = cause!!
247‚Üí        }
248‚Üí        return result
249‚Üí    }
250‚Üí
251‚Üí    /**
252‚Üí     * Send an exception event out for aggregation/analysis
253‚Üí     *
254‚Üí     * @param description API limited to 100 characters, truncated here to 100 if needed
255‚Üí     * @param fatal       whether it was fatal or not
256‚Üí     */
257‚Üí    fun sendAnalyticsException(description: String, fatal: Boolean) {
258‚Üí        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsException() description/fatal: %s/%s", description, fatal)
259‚Üí        if (!sOptIn) {
260‚Üí            return
261‚Üí        }
262‚Üí        sAnalytics!!.exception().exceptionDescription(description).exceptionFatal(fatal).sendAsync()
263‚Üí    }

265‚Üí    internal fun canGetDefaultUserAgent(): Boolean {
266‚Üí        // #5502 - getDefaultUserAgent starts a WebView. We can't have two WebViews with the same data directory.
267‚Üí        // But ACRA starts an :acra process which does not terminate when AnkiDroid is restarted. https://crbug.com/558377
268‚Üí
269‚Üí        // if we're not under the ACRA process then we're fine to initialize a WebView
270‚Üí        return if (!ACRA.isACRASenderServiceProcess()) {
271‚Üí            true
272‚Üí        } else {
273‚Üí            hasSetDataDirectory()
274‚Üí        }
275‚Üí
276‚Üí        // If we have a custom data directory, then the crash will not occur.
277‚Üí    }

279‚Üí    // A listener on this preference handles the rest
280‚Üí    var isEnabled: Boolean
281‚Üí        get() {
282‚Üí            val userPrefs = AnkiDroidApp.instance.sharedPrefs()
283‚Üí            return userPrefs.getBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, false)
284‚Üí        }
285‚Üí        set(value) {
286‚Üí            // A listener on this preference handles the rest
287‚Üí            AnkiDroidApp.instance.sharedPrefs().edit {
288‚Üí                putBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, value)
289‚Üí            }
290‚Üí        }

297‚Üí    /**
298‚Üí     * An Android-specific device config generator. Without this it's "Desktop" and unknown for all hardware.
299‚Üí     * It is interesting to us what devices people use though (for instance: is Amazon Kindle support worth it?
300‚Üí     * Is anyone still using e-ink devices? How many people are on tablets? ChromeOS?)
301‚Üí     */
302‚Üí    private class AndroidDefaultRequest : DefaultRequest() {
303‚Üí        fun setAndroidRequestParameters(context: Context): DefaultRequest {
304‚Üí            // Are we running on really large screens or small screens? Send raw screen size
305‚Üí            try {
306‚Üí                val size = DisplayUtils.getDisplayDimensions(context)
307‚Üí                this.screenResolution(size.x.toString() + "x" + size.y)
308‚Üí            } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
309‚Üí                Timber.w(e)
310‚Üí                // nothing much to do here, it means we couldn't get WindowManager
311‚Üí            }
312‚Üí
313‚Üí            // We can have up to 20 of these - there might be other things we want to know
314‚Üí            // but simply seeing what hardware we are running on should be useful
315‚Üí            this.customDimension(1, Build.VERSION.RELEASE) // systemVersion, e.g. "7.1.1"  for Android 7.1.1
316‚Üí            this.customDimension(2, Build.BRAND) // brand e.g. "OnePlus"
317‚Üí            this.customDimension(3, Build.MODEL) // model e.g. "ONEPLUS A6013" for the 6T
318‚Üí            this.customDimension(4, Build.BOARD) // deviceId e.g. "sdm845" for the 6T
319‚Üí
320‚Üí            // This is important for google to auto-fingerprint us for default reporting
321‚Üí            // It is not possible to set operating system explicitly, there is no API or analytics parameter for it
322‚Üí            // Instead they respond that they auto-parse User-Agent strings for analytics attribution
323‚Üí            // For maximum analytics built-in report compatibility we will send the official WebView User-Agent string
324‚Üí            try {
325‚Üí                if (canGetDefaultUserAgent()) {
326‚Üí                    this.userAgent(WebSettings.getDefaultUserAgent(context))
327‚Üí                } else {
328‚Üí                    this.userAgent(System.getProperty("http.agent"))
329‚Üí                }
330‚Üí            } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
331‚Üí                Timber.w(e)
332‚Üí                // Catch RuntimeException as WebView initialization blows up in unpredictable ways
333‚Üí                // but analytics should never be a show-stopper
334‚Üí                this.userAgent(System.getProperty("http.agent"))
335‚Üí            }
336‚Üí            return this
337‚Üí        }
338‚Üí    }

 13‚Üí *  You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along with
 14‚Üí *  this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
 15‚Üí */
 16‚Üí
 17‚Üípackage com.ichi2.anki.reviewer
 18‚Üí
 19‚Üíimport android.content.SharedPreferences
 20‚Üíimport android.view.Menu
 21‚Üíimport android.view.MenuItem
 22‚Üíimport androidx.annotation.IdRes
 23‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.R
 24‚Üíimport com.ichi2.utils.HashUtil.hashMapInit
 25‚Üí
 26‚Üí// loads of unboxing issues, which are safe
 27‚Üíclass ActionButtonStatus {
 28‚Üí    /**
 29‚Üí     * Custom button allocation
 30‚Üí     */
 31‚Üí    private val customButtons: MutableMap<Int, Int> = hashMapInit(25) // setup's size
 32‚Üí
 33‚Üí    fun setup(preferences: SharedPreferences) {
 34‚Üí        // NOTE: the default values below should be in sync with preferences_custom_buttons.xml and reviewer.xml
 35‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_undo, "customButtonUndo", SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS)
 36‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_redo, "customButtonRedo", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 37‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_schedule, "customButtonScheduleCard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 38‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_flag, "customButtonFlag", SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS)
 39‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_tag, "customButtonTags", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 40‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_edit, "customButtonEditCard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 41‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_add_note_reviewer, "customButtonAddCard", MENU_DISABLED)
 42‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_replay, "customButtonReplay", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 43‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_card_info, "customButtonCardInfo", MENU_DISABLED)
 44‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_clear_whiteboard, "customButtonClearWhiteboard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 45‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_hide_whiteboard, "customButtonShowHideWhiteboard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS)
 46‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_select_tts, "customButtonSelectTts", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 47‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_open_deck_options, "customButtonDeckOptions", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 48‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_bury, "customButtonBury", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 49‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_bury_card, "customButtonBury", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 50‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_suspend, "customButtonSuspend", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 51‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_suspend_card, "customButtonSuspend", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 52‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_mark_card, "customButtonMarkCard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 53‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_delete, "customButtonDelete", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 54‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_toggle_mic_tool_bar, "customButtonToggleMicToolBar", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 55‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_toggle_whiteboard, "customButtonEnableWhiteboard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 56‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_toggle_stylus, "customButtonToggleStylus", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 57‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_save_whiteboard, "customButtonSaveWhiteboard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 58‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_change_whiteboard_pen_color, "customButtonWhiteboardPenColor", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 59‚Üí    }
 60‚Üí
 61‚Üí    private fun setupButton(preferences: SharedPreferences, @IdRes resourceId: Int, preferenceName: String, showAsActionType: Int) {
 62‚Üí        customButtons[resourceId] = preferences.getString(
 63‚Üí            preferenceName,
 64‚Üí            showAsActionType.toString()
 65‚Üí        )!!.toInt()
 66‚Üí    }
 67‚Üí
 68‚Üí    fun setCustomButtons(menu: Menu) {
 69‚Üí        for ((itemId, value) in customButtons) {
 70‚Üí            if (value != MENU_DISABLED) {
 71‚Üí                val item = menu.findItem(itemId)
 72‚Üí                item.setShowAsAction(value)
 73‚Üí            } else {
 74‚Üí                menu.findItem(itemId).isVisible = false
 75‚Üí            }
 76‚Üí        }
 77‚Üí    }
 78‚Üí
 79‚Üí    fun hideWhiteboardIsDisabled(): Boolean {
 80‚Üí        return customButtons[R.id.action_hide_whiteboard] == MENU_DISABLED
 81‚Üí    }
 82‚Üí
 83‚Üí    fun toggleStylusIsDisabled(): Boolean {
 84‚Üí        return customButtons[R.id.action_toggle_stylus] == MENU_DISABLED
 85‚Üí    }
 86‚Üí
 87‚Üí    fun clearWhiteboardIsDisabled(): Boolean {
 88‚Üí        return customButtons[R.id.action_clear_whiteboard] == MENU_DISABLED
 89‚Üí    }
 90‚Üí
 91‚Üí    fun selectTtsIsDisabled(): Boolean {
 92‚Üí        return customButtons[R.id.action_select_tts] == MENU_DISABLED
 93‚Üí    }
 94‚Üí
 95‚Üí    fun saveWhiteboardIsDisabled(): Boolean {
 96‚Üí        return customButtons[R.id.action_save_whiteboard] == MENU_DISABLED
 97‚Üí    }
 98‚Üí
 99‚Üí    fun whiteboardPenColorIsDisabled(): Boolean {
100‚Üí        return customButtons[R.id.action_change_whiteboard_pen_color] == MENU_DISABLED
101‚Üí    }
102‚Üí
103‚Üí    fun suspendIsDisabled(): Boolean = customButtons[R.id.action_suspend] == MENU_DISABLED
104‚Üí
105‚Üí    fun buryIsDisabled(): Boolean = customButtons[R.id.action_bury] == MENU_DISABLED
106‚Üí
107‚Üí    companion object {
108‚Üí        const val SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER = MenuItem.SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER
109‚Üí        const val SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM = MenuItem.SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM
110‚Üí        const val SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS = MenuItem.SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS
111‚Üí        const val MENU_DISABLED = 3
112‚Üí    }
113‚Üí}
114‚Üí

 78‚Üí    open fun convertToByteArray(apiContract: ApiContract, boolean: Boolean): ByteArray {
 79‚Üí        return ApiResult(apiContract.isValid, boolean.toString()).toString().toByteArray()
 80‚Üí    }
 81‚Üí
 82‚Üí    open fun convertToByteArray(apiContract: ApiContract, int: Int): ByteArray {
 83‚Üí        return ApiResult(apiContract.isValid, int.toString()).toString().toByteArray()
 84‚Üí    }
 85‚Üí
 86‚Üí    open fun convertToByteArray(apiContract: ApiContract, long: Long): ByteArray {
 87‚Üí        return ApiResult(apiContract.isValid, long.toString()).toString().toByteArray()
 88‚Üí    }
 89‚Üí
 90‚Üí    open fun convertToByteArray(apiContract: ApiContract, string: String): ByteArray {
 91‚Üí        return ApiResult(apiContract.isValid, string).toString().toByteArray()
 92‚Üí    }
 93‚Üí
 94‚Üí    /**
 95‚Üí     * The method parse json data and return api contract object
 96‚Üí     * @param byteArray
 97‚Üí     * @return apiContract or null
 98‚Üí     */
 99‚Üí    private fun parseJsApiContract(byteArray: ByteArray): ApiContract? {
100‚Üí        try {
101‚Üí            val data = JSONObject(byteArray.decodeToString())
102‚Üí            val cardSuppliedApiVersion = data.optString("version", "")
103‚Üí            val cardSuppliedDeveloperContact = data.optString("developer", "")
104‚Üí            val cardSuppliedData = data.optString("data", "")
105‚Üí            val isValid = requireApiVersion(cardSuppliedApiVersion, cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
106‚Üí            return ApiContract(isValid, cardSuppliedDeveloperContact, cardSuppliedData)
107‚Üí        } catch (j: JSONException) {
108‚Üí            Timber.w(j)
109‚Üí            activity.runOnUiThread {
110‚Üí                activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.invalid_json_data, j.localizedMessage))
111‚Üí            }
112‚Üí        }
113‚Üí        return null
114‚Üí    }
115‚Üí
116‚Üí    /*
117‚Üí     * see 02-strings.xml
118‚Üí     * Show Error code when mark card or flag card unsupported
119‚Üí     * 1 - mark card
120‚Üí     * 2 - flag card
121‚Üí     *
122‚Üí     * show developer contact if js api used in card is deprecated
123‚Üí     */
124‚Üí    private fun showDeveloperContact(errorCode: Int, apiDevContact: String) {
125‚Üí        val errorMsg: String = context.getString(R.string.anki_js_error_code, errorCode)
126‚Üí        val snackbarMsg: String = context.getString(R.string.api_version_developer_contact, apiDevContact, errorMsg)
127‚Üí
128‚Üí        activity.showSnackbar(snackbarMsg, Snackbar.LENGTH_INDEFINITE) {
129‚Üí            setMaxLines(3)
130‚Üí            setAction(R.string.reviewer_invalid_api_version_visit_documentation) {
131‚Üí                activity.openUrl(Uri.parse("https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki"))
132‚Üí            }
133‚Üí        }
134‚Üí    }
135‚Üí
136‚Üí    /**
137‚Üí     * Supplied api version must be equal to current api version to call mark card, toggle flag functions etc.
138‚Üí     */
139‚Üí    private fun requireApiVersion(apiVer: String, apiDevContact: String): Boolean {
140‚Üí        try {
141‚Üí            if (apiDevContact.isEmpty() || apiVer.isEmpty()) {
142‚Üí                activity.runOnUiThread {
143‚Üí                    activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.invalid_json_data, ""))
144‚Üí                }
145‚Üí                return false
146‚Üí            }
147‚Üí            val versionCurrent = Version.parse(AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.sCurrentJsApiVersion)
148‚Üí            val versionSupplied = Version.parse(apiVer)
149‚Üí
150‚Üí            /*
151‚Üí            * if api major version equals to supplied major version then return true and also check for minor version and patch version
152‚Üí            * show toast for update and contact developer if need updates
153‚Üí            * otherwise return false
154‚Üí            */
155‚Üí            return when {
156‚Üí                versionSupplied == versionCurrent -> {
157‚Üí                    true
158‚Üí                }
159‚Üí                versionSupplied.isLowerThan(versionCurrent) -> {
160‚Üí                    activity.runOnUiThread {
161‚Üí                        activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.update_js_api_version, apiDevContact))
162‚Üí                    }
163‚Üí                    versionSupplied.isHigherThanOrEquivalentTo(Version.parse(AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.sMinimumJsApiVersion))
164‚Üí                }
165‚Üí                else -> {
166‚Üí                    activity.runOnUiThread {
167‚Üí                        activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.valid_js_api_version, apiDevContact))
168‚Üí                    }
169‚Üí                    false
170‚Üí                }
171‚Üí            }
172‚Üí        } catch (e: Exception) {
173‚Üí            Timber.w(e, "requireApiVersion::exception")
174‚Üí        }
175‚Üí        return false
176‚Üí    }
177‚Üí
178‚Üí    /**
179‚Üí     * Handle js api request,
180‚Üí     * some of the methods are overridden in Reviewer.kt and default values are returned.
181‚Üí     * @param methodName
182‚Üí     * @param bytes
183‚Üí     * @param returnDefaultValues `true` if default values should be returned (if non-[Reviewer])
184‚Üí     * @return
185‚Üí     */
186‚Üí    open suspend fun handleJsApiRequest(methodName: String, bytes: ByteArray, returnDefaultValues: Boolean = true) = withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
187‚Üí        // the method will call to set the card supplied data and is valid version for each api request
188‚Üí        val apiContract = parseJsApiContract(bytes)!!
189‚Üí        // if api not init or is api not called from reviewer then return default -1
190‚Üí        // also other action will not be modified
191‚Üí        if (!apiContract.isValid or returnDefaultValues) {
192‚Üí            return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, -1)
193‚Üí        }
194‚Üí
195‚Üí        val cardDataForJsAPI = activity.getCardDataForJsApi()
196‚Üí        val apiParams = apiContract.cardSuppliedData
197‚Üí
198‚Üí        return@withContext when (methodName) {
199‚Üí            "init" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
200‚Üí            "newCardCount" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.newCardCount)
201‚Üí            "lrnCardCount" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.lrnCardCount)
202‚Üí            "revCardCount" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.revCardCount)
203‚Üí            "eta" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.eta)
204‚Üí            "nextTime1" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime1)
205‚Üí            "nextTime2" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime2)
206‚Üí            "nextTime3" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime3)
207‚Üí            "nextTime4" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime4)
208‚Üí            "toggleFlag" -> {
209‚Üí                if (apiParams !in flagCommands) {
210‚Üí                    showDeveloperContact(ankiJsErrorCodeFlagCard, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
211‚Üí                    return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
212‚Üí                }
213‚Üí                convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.executeCommand(flagCommands[apiParams]!!))
214‚Üí            }
215‚Üí            "markCard" -> processAction({ activity.executeCommand(ViewerCommand.MARK) }, apiContract, ankiJsErrorCodeMarkCard, ::convertToByteArray)
216‚Üí            "buryCard" -> processAction(activity::buryCard, apiContract, ankiJsErrorCodeBuryCard, ::convertToByteArray)
217‚Üí            "buryNote" -> processAction(activity::buryNote, apiContract, ankiJsErrorCodeBuryNote, ::convertToByteArray)
218‚Üí            "suspendCard" -> processAction(activity::suspendCard, apiContract, ankiJsErrorCodeSuspendCard, ::convertToByteArray)
219‚Üí            "suspendNote" -> processAction(activity::suspendNote, apiContract, ankiJsErrorCodeSuspendNote, ::convertToByteArray)
220‚Üí            "setCardDue" -> {
221‚Üí                try {
222‚Üí                    val days = apiParams.toInt()
223‚Üí                    if (days < 0 || days > 9999) {
224‚Üí                        showDeveloperContact(ankiJsErrorCodeSetDue, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
225‚Üí                        return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
226‚Üí                    }
227‚Üí                    activity.launchCatchingTask {
228‚Üí                        activity.rescheduleCards(listOf(currentCard.id), days)
229‚Üí                    }
230‚Üí                    return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
231‚Üí                } catch (e: NumberFormatException) {
232‚Üí                    showDeveloperContact(ankiJsErrorCodeSetDue, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
233‚Üí                    return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
234‚Üí                }
235‚Üí            }
236‚Üí            "resetProgress" -> {
237‚Üí                val cardIds = listOf(currentCard.id)
238‚Üí                activity.launchCatchingTask { activity.resetCards(cardIds) }
239‚Üí                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
240‚Üí            }
241‚Üí            "cardMark" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.note(getColUnsafe).hasTag(getColUnsafe, "marked"))
242‚Üí            "cardFlag" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.userFlag())
243‚Üí            "cardReps" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.reps)
244‚Üí            "cardInterval" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.ivl)
245‚Üí            "cardFactor" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.factor)
246‚Üí            "cardMod" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.mod)
247‚Üí            "cardId" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.id)
248‚Üí            "cardNid" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.nid)
249‚Üí            "cardType" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.type)
250‚Üí            "cardDid" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.did)
251‚Üí            "cardLeft" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.left)
252‚Üí            "cardODid" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.oDid)
253‚Üí            "cardODue" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.oDue)
254‚Üí            "cardQueue" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.queue)
255‚Üí            "cardLapses" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.lapses)
256‚Üí            "cardDue" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.due)
257‚Üí            "deckName" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, Decks.basename(activity.getColUnsafe.decks.name(currentCard.did)))
258‚Üí            "isActiveNetworkMetered" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, NetworkUtils.isActiveNetworkMetered())
259‚Üí            "ttsSetLanguage" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.setLanguage(apiParams))
260‚Üí            "ttsSpeak" -> {
261‚Üí                val jsonObject = JSONObject(apiParams)
262‚Üí                val text = jsonObject.getString("text")
263‚Üí                val queueMode = jsonObject.getInt("queueMode")
264‚Üí                convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.speak(text, queueMode))
265‚Üí            }
266‚Üí            "ttsIsSpeaking" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.isSpeaking)
267‚Üí            "ttsSetPitch" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.setPitch(apiParams.toFloat()))
268‚Üí            "ttsSetSpeechRate" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.setSpeechRate(apiParams.toFloat()))
269‚Üí            "ttsFieldModifierIsAvailable" -> {
270‚Üí                // Know if {{tts}} is supported - issue #10443
271‚Üí                // Return false for now
272‚Üí                convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
273‚Üí            }
274‚Üí            "ttsStop" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.stop())
275‚Üí            "searchCard" -> {
276‚Üí                val intent = Intent(context, CardBrowser::class.java).apply {
277‚Üí                    putExtra("currentCard", currentCard.id)
278‚Üí                    putExtra("search_query", apiParams)
279‚Üí                }
280‚Üí                activity.startActivity(intent)
281‚Üí                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
282‚Üí            }
283‚Üí            "searchCardWithCallback" -> ankiSearchCardWithCallback(apiContract)
284‚Üí            "isDisplayingAnswer" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.isDisplayingAnswer)
285‚Üí            "addTagToCard" -> {
286‚Üí                activity.runOnUiThread { activity.showTagsDialog() }
287‚Üí                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
288‚Üí            }
289‚Üí            "isInFullscreen" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.isFullscreen)
290‚Üí            "isTopbarShown" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.prefShowTopbar)
291‚Üí            "isInNightMode" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.isInNightMode)
292‚Üí            "enableHorizontalScrollbar" -> {
293‚Üí                activity.webView!!.isHorizontalScrollBarEnabled = apiParams.toBoolean()
294‚Üí                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
295‚Üí            }
296‚Üí            "enableVerticalScrollbar" -> {
297‚Üí                activity.webView!!.isVerticalScrollBarEnabled = apiParams.toBoolean()
298‚Üí                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
299‚Üí            }
300‚Üí            "showNavigationDrawer" -> {
301‚Üí                activity.onNavigationPressed()
302‚Üí                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
303‚Üí            }
304‚Üí            "showOptionsMenu" -> {
305‚Üí                activity.openOptionsMenu()
306‚Üí                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
307‚Üí            }
308‚Üí            "showToast" -> {
309‚Üí                val jsonObject = JSONObject(apiParams)
310‚Üí                val text = jsonObject.getString("text")
311‚Üí                val shortLength = jsonObject.optBoolean("shortLength", true)
312‚Üí                val msgDecode = activity.decodeUrl(text)
313‚Üí                UIUtils.showThemedToast(context, msgDecode, shortLength)
314‚Üí                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
315‚Üí            }
316‚Üí            "showAnswer" -> {
317‚Üí                activity.displayCardAnswer()
318‚Üí                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
319‚Üí            }
320‚Üí            "answerEase1" -> {
321‚Üí                activity.flipOrAnswerCard(AbstractFlashcardViewer.EASE_1)
322‚Üí                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
323‚Üí            }
324‚Üí            "answerEase2" -> {
325‚Üí                activity.flipOrAnswerCard(AbstractFlashcardViewer.EASE_2)
326‚Üí                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
327‚Üí            }
328‚Üí            "answerEase3" -> {
329‚Üí                activity.flipOrAnswerCard(AbstractFlashcardViewer.EASE_3)
330‚Üí                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
331‚Üí            }
332‚Üí            "answerEase4" -> {
333‚Üí                activity.flipOrAnswerCard(AbstractFlashcardViewer.EASE_4)
334‚Üí                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
335‚Üí            }
336‚Üí            "sttSetLanguage" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, mSpeechRecognizer.setLanguage(apiParams))
337‚Üí            "sttStart" -> {
338‚Üí                val callback = object : JavaScriptSTT.SpeechRecognitionCallback {
339‚Üí                    override fun onResult(results: List<String>) {
340‚Üí                        activity.lifecycleScope.launch {
341‚Üí                            val apiResult = ApiResult(true, Json.encodeToString(ListSerializer(String.serializer()), results))
342‚Üí                            val jsonEncodedString = withContext(Dispatchers.Default) { JSONObject.quote(apiResult.toString()) }
343‚Üí                            activity.webView!!.evaluateJavascript("ankiSttResult($jsonEncodedString)", null)
344‚Üí                        }
345‚Üí                    }
346‚Üí                    override fun onError(errorMessage: String) {
347‚Üí                        activity.lifecycleScope.launch {
348‚Üí                            val apiResult = ApiResult(false, errorMessage)
349‚Üí                            val jsonEncodedString = withContext(Dispatchers.Default) { JSONObject.quote(apiResult.toString()) }
350‚Üí                            activity.webView!!.evaluateJavascript("ankiSttResult($jsonEncodedString)", null)
351‚Üí                        }
352‚Üí                    }
353‚Üí                }
354‚Üí                mSpeechRecognizer.setRecognitionCallback(callback)
355‚Üí                convertToByteArray(apiContract, mSpeechRecognizer.start())
356‚Üí            }
357‚Üí            "sttStop" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, mSpeechRecognizer.stop())
358‚Üí            else -> {
359‚Üí                showDeveloperContact(ankiJsErrorCodeError, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
360‚Üí                throw Exception("unhandled request: $methodName")
361‚Üí            }
362‚Üí        }
363‚Üí    }
364‚Üí
365‚Üí    private fun processAction(
366‚Üí        action: () -> Boolean,
367‚Üí        apiContract: ApiContract,
368‚Üí        errorCode: Int,
369‚Üí        conversion: (ApiContract, Boolean) -> ByteArray
370‚Üí    ): ByteArray {
371‚Üí        val status = action()
372‚Üí        if (!status) {
373‚Üí            showDeveloperContact(errorCode, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
374‚Üí        }
375‚Üí        return conversion(apiContract, status)
376‚Üí    }
377‚Üí
378‚Üí    private suspend fun ankiSearchCardWithCallback(apiContract: ApiContract): ByteArray = withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
379‚Üí        val cards = try {
380‚Üí            searchForCards(apiContract.cardSuppliedData, SortOrder.UseCollectionOrdering(), CardsOrNotes.CARDS)
381‚Üí        } catch (exc: Exception) {
382‚Üí            activity.webView!!.evaluateJavascript(
383‚Üí                "console.log('${context.getString(R.string.search_card_js_api_no_results)}')",
384‚Üí                null
385‚Üí            )
386‚Üí            showDeveloperContact(AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.ankiJsErrorCodeSearchCard, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
387‚Üí            return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
388‚Üí        }
389‚Üí        val searchResult: MutableList<String> = ArrayList()
390‚Üí        for (s in cards) {
391‚Üí            val jsonObject = JSONObject()
392‚Üí            val fieldsData = s.card.note(getColUnsafe).fields
393‚Üí            val fieldsName = s.card.model(getColUnsafe).fieldsNames
394‚Üí
395‚Üí            val noteId = s.card.nid
396‚Üí            val cardId = s.card.id
397‚Üí            jsonObject.put("cardId", cardId)
398‚Üí            jsonObject.put("noteId", noteId)
399‚Üí
400‚Üí            val jsonFieldObject = JSONObject()
401‚Üí            fieldsName.zip(fieldsData).forEach { pair ->
402‚Üí                jsonFieldObject.put(pair.component1(), pair.component2())
403‚Üí            }
404‚Üí            jsonObject.put("fieldsData", jsonFieldObject)
405‚Üí
406‚Üí            searchResult.add(jsonObject.toString())
407‚Üí        }
408‚Üí
409‚Üí        // quote result to prevent JSON injection attack
410‚Üí        val jsonEncodedString = JSONObject.quote(searchResult.toString())
411‚Üí        activity.runOnUiThread {
412‚Üí            activity.webView!!.evaluateJavascript("ankiSearchCard($jsonEncodedString)", null)
413‚Üí        }
414‚Üí        convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
415‚Üí    }
416‚Üí
417‚Üí    open class CardDataForJsApi {
418‚Üí        var newCardCount = ""
419‚Üí        var lrnCardCount = ""
420‚Üí        var revCardCount = ""
421‚Üí        var eta = -1
422‚Üí        var nextTime1 = ""
423‚Üí        var nextTime2 = ""
424‚Üí        var nextTime3 = ""
425‚Üí        var nextTime4 = ""
426‚Üí    }
427‚Üí
428‚Üí    class ApiResult(private val status: Boolean, private val value: String) {
429‚Üí        override fun toString(): String {
430‚Üí            return JSONObject().apply {
431‚Üí                put("success", status)
432‚Üí                put("value", value)
433‚Üí            }.toString()
434‚Üí        }
435‚Üí    }
436‚Üí
437‚Üí    class ApiContract(val isValid: Boolean, val cardSuppliedDeveloperContact: String, val cardSuppliedData: String)
438‚Üí}
439‚Üí

content.text:
I have implemented the 'User Action' functionality in AnkiDroid, similar to AnkiMobile. This feature allows users to trigger JavaScript functions defined in their card templates (e.g., `var userJs1 = revealNext;`) using gestures, keyboard shortcuts, or external devices.

Key changes:
1.  **`ViewerCommand.kt`**: Added `USER_ACTION_1` through `USER_ACTION_8` to the command enum.
2.  **`card.js`**: Added a `runUserAction(actionNum)` JavaScript function to the card assets. This function checks for the presence of a `userJsX` variable and calls it if it is a function.
3.  **`AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt`**: Implemented handling for `USER_ACTION_X` commands, which triggers the corresponding JavaScript function in the WebView.
4.  **`Reviewer.kt`**: Added support for triggering these actions from the app bar menu.
5.  **`ActionButtonStatus.kt`**: Registered the new user actions so they can be customized (shown/hidden) in the app bar.
6.  **`UsageAnalytics.kt`**: Included the new command and custom button settings in the analytics tracking.

Users can now map these actions in the "Reviewing > Gestures" or "Reviewing > App bar buttons" settings. For example, to use a "reveal next" functionality from a shared deck, a user can assign a gesture to "User action 1" and ensured their template has `var userJs1 = revealNext;`.

 88‚Üí        get() = "binding_$name"
 89‚Üí
 90‚Üí    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 91‚Üí        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 92‚Üí            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
 93‚Üí            collection.remove(element)
 94‚Üí            collection.add(0, element)
 95‚Üí            true
 96‚Üí        }
 97‚Üí        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
 98‚Üí    }
 99‚Üí
100‚Üí    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
101‚Üí        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
102‚Üí            // do not reorder the elements
103‚Üí            if (collection.contains(element)) {
104‚Üí                return@BiFunction false
105‚Üí            }
106‚Üí            collection.add(element)
107‚Üí            return@BiFunction true
108‚Üí        }
109‚Üí        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
110‚Üí    }
111‚Üí
112‚Üí    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
113‚Üí        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
114‚Üí        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
115‚Üí        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
116‚Üí        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
117‚Üí    }
118‚Üí
119‚Üí    fun removeBinding(prefs: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
120‚Üí        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
121‚Üí        bindings.remove(binding)
122‚Üí        prefs.edit {
123‚Üí            putString(preferenceKey, bindings.toPreferenceString())
124‚Üí        }
125‚Üí    }

 44‚Üí    @KotlinCleanup("lateinit")

 51‚Üí    /**
 52‚Üí     * Initialize the analytics provider - must be called prior to sending anything.
 53‚Üí     * Usage after that is static
 54‚Üí     * Note: may need to implement sampling strategy internally to limit hits, or not track Reviewer...
 55‚Üí     *
 56‚Üí     * @param context required to look up the analytics codes for the app
 57‚Üí     */
 58‚Üí    @Synchronized
 59‚Üí    fun initialize(context: Context): GoogleAnalytics? {
 60‚Üí        Timber.i("initialize()")
 61‚Üí        if (sAnalytics == null) {
 62‚Üí            Timber.d("App tracking id 'tid' = %s", getAnalyticsTag(context))
 63‚Üí            val gaConfig = GoogleAnalyticsConfig()
 64‚Üí                .setBatchingEnabled(true)
 65‚Üí                .setSamplePercentage(getAnalyticsSamplePercentage(context))
 66‚Üí                .setBatchSize(1) // until this handles application termination we will lose hits if batch>1
 67‚Üí            sAnalytics = GoogleAnalytics.builder()
 68‚Üí                .withTrackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 69‚Üí                .withConfig(gaConfig)
 70‚Üí                .withDefaultRequest(
 71‚Üí                    AndroidDefaultRequest()
 72‚Üí                        .setAndroidRequestParameters(context)
 73‚Üí                        .applicationName(context.getString(R.string.app_name))
 74‚Üí                        .applicationVersion(BuildConfig.VERSION_CODE.toString())
 75‚Üí                        .applicationId(BuildConfig.APPLICATION_ID)
 76‚Üí                        .trackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 77‚Üí                        .clientId(Installation.id(context))
 78‚Üí                        .anonymizeIp(context.resources.getBoolean(R.bool.ga_anonymizeIp))
 79‚Üí                )
 80‚Üí                .withHttpClient(OkHttpClientImpl(gaConfig))
 81‚Üí                .build()
 82‚Üí        }
 83‚Üí        installDefaultExceptionHandler()
 84‚Üí        val userPrefs = context.sharedPrefs()
 85‚Üí        optIn = userPrefs.getBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, false)
 86‚Üí        userPrefs.registerOnSharedPreferenceChangeListener { sharedPreferences: SharedPreferences, key: String? ->
 87‚Üí            if (key == ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY) {
 88‚Üí                val newValue = sharedPreferences.getBoolean(key, false)
 89‚Üí                Timber.i("Setting analytics opt-in to: %b", newValue)
 90‚Üí                optIn = newValue
 91‚Üí            }
 92‚Üí        }
 93‚Üí        return sAnalytics
 94‚Üí    }
 95‚Üí
 96‚Üí    private fun getAnalyticsTag(context: Context): String? {
 97‚Üí        if (sAnalyticsTrackingId == null) {
 98‚Üí            sAnalyticsTrackingId = context.getString(R.string.ga_trackingId)
 99‚Üí        }
100‚Üí        return sAnalyticsTrackingId
101‚Üí    }
102‚Üí
103‚Üí    private fun getAnalyticsSamplePercentage(context: Context): Int {
104‚Üí        if (sAnalyticsSamplePercentage == -1) {
105‚Üí            sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = context.resources.getInteger(R.integer.ga_sampleFrequency)
106‚Üí        }
107‚Üí        return sAnalyticsSamplePercentage
108‚Üí    }

117‚Üí    /**
118‚Üí     * We want to send an analytics hit on any exception, then chain to other handlers (e.g., ACRA)
119‚Üí     */
120‚Üí    @Synchronized
121‚Üí    private fun installDefaultExceptionHandler() {
122‚Üí        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = Thread.getDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler()
123‚Üí        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler { thread: Thread?, throwable: Throwable ->
124‚Üí            sendAnalyticsException(throwable, true)
125‚Üí            if (thread == null) {
126‚Üí                Timber.w("unexpected: thread was null")
127‚Üí                return@setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler
128‚Üí            }
129‚Üí            sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler!!.uncaughtException(thread, throwable)
130‚Üí        }
131‚Üí    }
132‚Üí
133‚Üí    /**
134‚Üí     * Reset the default exception handler
135‚Üí     */
136‚Üí    @Synchronized
137‚Üí    private fun unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler() {
138‚Üí        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler(sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler)
139‚Üí        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = null
140‚Üí    }
141‚Üí
142‚Üí    /**
143‚Üí     * Allow users to enable or disable analytics
144‚Üí     */
145‚Üí    @set:Synchronized
146‚Üí    private var optIn: Boolean
147‚Üí        get() {
148‚Üí            Timber.d("getOptIn() status: %s", sOptIn)
149‚Üí            return sOptIn
150‚Üí        }
151‚Üí        private set(optIn) {
152‚Üí            Timber.i("setOptIn(): from %s to %s", sOptIn, optIn)
153‚Üí            sOptIn = optIn
154‚Üí            sAnalytics!!.flush()
155‚Üí            sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled = optIn
156‚Üí            sAnalytics!!.performSamplingElection()
157‚Üí            Timber.d("setOptIn() optIn / sAnalytics.config().enabled(): %s/%s", sOptIn, sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled)
158‚Üí        }

170‚Üí    /**
171‚Üí     * Re-Initialize the analytics provider
172‚Üí     */
173‚Üí    @Synchronized
174‚Üí    fun reInitialize() {
175‚Üí        // send any pending async hits, re-chain default exception handlers and re-init
176‚Üí        Timber.i("reInitialize()")
177‚Üí        sAnalytics!!.flush()
178‚Üí        sAnalytics = null
179‚Üí        unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler()
180‚Üí        initialize(AnkiDroidApp.instance.applicationContext)
181‚Üí    }

183‚Üí    /**
184‚Üí     * Submit a screen for aggregation / analysis.
185‚Üí     * Intended for use to determine if / how features are being used
186‚Üí     *
187‚Üí     * @param object the result of Object.getClass().getSimpleName() will be used as the screen tag
188‚Üí     */
189‚Üí    @KotlinCleanup("rename object")
190‚Üí    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(`object`: Any) {
191‚Üí        sendAnalyticsScreenView(`object`.javaClass.simpleName)
192‚Üí    }
193‚Üí
194‚Üí    /**
195‚Üí     * Submit a screen display with a synthetic name for aggregation / analysis
196‚Üí     * Intended for use if your class handles multiple screens you want to track separately
197‚Üí     *
198‚Üí     * @param screenName screenName the name to show in analysis reports
199‚Üí     */
200‚Üí    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(screenName: String) {
201‚Üí        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsScreenView(): %s", screenName)
202‚Üí        if (!optIn) {
203‚Üí            return
204‚Üí        }
205‚Üí        sAnalytics!!.screenView().screenName(screenName).sendAsync()
206‚Üí    }
207‚Üí
208‚Üí    /**
209‚Üí     * Send a detailed arbitrary analytics event, with noun/verb pairs and extra data if needed
210‚Üí     *
211‚Üí     * @param category the category of event, make your own but use a constant so reporting is good
212‚Üí     * @param action   the action the user performed
213‚Üí     * @param value    A value for the event, Integer.MIN_VALUE signifies caller shouldn't send the value
214‚Üí     * @param label    A label for the event, may be null
215‚Üí     */
216‚Üí    fun sendAnalyticsEvent(category: String, action: String, value: Int? = null, label: String? = null) {
217‚Üí        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsEvent() category/action/value/label: %s/%s/%s/%s", category, action, value, label)
218‚Üí        if (!optIn) {
219‚Üí            return
220‚Üí        }
221‚Üí        val event = sAnalytics!!.event().eventCategory(category).eventAction(action)
222‚Üí        if (label != null) {
223‚Üí            event.eventLabel(label)
224‚Üí        }
225‚Üí        if (value != null) {
226‚Üí            event.eventValue(value)
227‚Üí        }
228‚Üí        event.sendAsync()
229‚Üí    }
230‚Üí
231‚Üí    /**
232‚Üí     * Send an exception event out for aggregation/analysis, parsed from the exception information
233‚Üí     *
234‚Üí     * @param t     Throwable to send for analysis
235‚Üí     * @param fatal whether it was fatal or not
236‚Üí     */
237‚Üí    fun sendAnalyticsException(t: Throwable, fatal: Boolean) {
238‚Üí        sendAnalyticsException(getCause(t).toString(), fatal)
239‚Üí    }
240‚Üí
241‚Üí    @KotlinCleanup("convert to sequence")
242‚Üí    fun getCause(t: Throwable): Throwable {
243‚Üí        var cause: Throwable?
244‚Üí        var result = t
245‚Üí        while (null != result.cause.also { cause = it } && result != cause) {
246‚Üí            result = cause!!
247‚Üí        }
248‚Üí        return result
249‚Üí    }
250‚Üí
251‚Üí    /**
252‚Üí     * Send an exception event out for aggregation/analysis
253‚Üí     *
254‚Üí     * @param description API limited to 100 characters, truncated here to 100 if needed
255‚Üí     * @param fatal       whether it was fatal or not
256‚Üí     */
257‚Üí    fun sendAnalyticsException(description: String, fatal: Boolean) {
258‚Üí        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsException() description/fatal: %s/%s", description, fatal)
259‚Üí        if (!sOptIn) {
260‚Üí            return
261‚Üí        }
262‚Üí        sAnalytics!!.exception().exceptionDescription(description).exceptionFatal(fatal).sendAsync()
263‚Üí    }

265‚Üí    internal fun canGetDefaultUserAgent(): Boolean {
266‚Üí        // #5502 - getDefaultUserAgent starts a WebView. We can't have two WebViews with the same data directory.
267‚Üí        // But ACRA starts an :acra process which does not terminate when AnkiDroid is restarted. https://crbug.com/558377
268‚Üí
269‚Üí        // if we're not under the ACRA process then we're fine to initialize a WebView
270‚Üí        return if (!ACRA.isACRASenderServiceProcess()) {
271‚Üí            true
272‚Üí        } else {
273‚Üí            hasSetDataDirectory()
274‚Üí        }
275‚Üí
276‚Üí        // If we have a custom data directory, then the crash will not occur.
277‚Üí    }

279‚Üí    // A listener on this preference handles the rest
280‚Üí    var isEnabled: Boolean
281‚Üí        get() {
282‚Üí            val userPrefs = AnkiDroidApp.instance.sharedPrefs()
283‚Üí            return userPrefs.getBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, false)
284‚Üí        }
285‚Üí        set(value) {
286‚Üí            // A listener on this preference handles the rest
287‚Üí            AnkiDroidApp.instance.sharedPrefs().edit {
288‚Üí                putBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, value)
289‚Üí            }
290‚Üí        }

297‚Üí    /**
298‚Üí     * An Android-specific device config generator. Without this it's "Desktop" and unknown for all hardware.
299‚Üí     * It is interesting to us what devices people use though (for instance: is Amazon Kindle support worth it?
300‚Üí     * Is anyone still using e-ink devices? How many people are on tablets? ChromeOS?)
301‚Üí     */
302‚Üí    private class AndroidDefaultRequest : DefaultRequest() {
303‚Üí        fun setAndroidRequestParameters(context: Context): DefaultRequest {
304‚Üí            // Are we running on really large screens or small screens? Send raw screen size
305‚Üí            try {
306‚Üí                val size = DisplayUtils.getDisplayDimensions(context)
307‚Üí                this.screenResolution(size.x.toString() + "x" + size.y)
308‚Üí            } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
309‚Üí                Timber.w(e)
310‚Üí                // nothing much to do here, it means we couldn't get WindowManager
311‚Üí            }
312‚Üí
313‚Üí            // We can have up to 20 of these - there might be other things we want to know
314‚Üí            // but simply seeing what hardware we are running on should be useful
315‚Üí            this.customDimension(1, Build.VERSION.RELEASE) // systemVersion, e.g. "7.1.1"  for Android 7.1.1
316‚Üí            this.customDimension(2, Build.BRAND) // brand e.g. "OnePlus"
317‚Üí            this.customDimension(3, Build.MODEL) // model e.g. "ONEPLUS A6013" for the 6T
318‚Üí            this.customDimension(4, Build.BOARD) // deviceId e.g. "sdm845" for the 6T
319‚Üí
320‚Üí            // This is important for google to auto-fingerprint us for default reporting
321‚Üí            // It is not possible to set operating system explicitly, there is no API or analytics parameter for it
322‚Üí            // Instead they respond that they auto-parse User-Agent strings for analytics attribution
323‚Üí            // For maximum analytics built-in report compatibility we will send the official WebView User-Agent string
324‚Üí            try {
325‚Üí                if (canGetDefaultUserAgent()) {
326‚Üí                    this.userAgent(WebSettings.getDefaultUserAgent(context))
327‚Üí                } else {
328‚Üí                    this.userAgent(System.getProperty("http.agent"))
329‚Üí                }
330‚Üí            } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
331‚Üí                Timber.w(e)
332‚Üí                // Catch RuntimeException as WebView initialization blows up in unpredictable ways
333‚Üí                // but analytics should never be a show-stopper
334‚Üí                this.userAgent(System.getProperty("http.agent"))
335‚Üí            }
336‚Üí            return this
337‚Üí        }
338‚Üí    }

 17‚Üípackage com.ichi2.anki.reviewer
 18‚Üí
 19‚Üíimport android.content.SharedPreferences
 20‚Üíimport android.view.Menu
 21‚Üíimport android.view.MenuItem
 22‚Üíimport androidx.annotation.IdRes
 23‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.R
 24‚Üíimport com.ichi2.utils.HashUtil.hashMapInit
 25‚Üí
 26‚Üí// loads of unboxing issues, which are safe
 27‚Üíclass ActionButtonStatus {
 28‚Üí    /**
 29‚Üí     * Custom button allocation
 30‚Üí     */
 31‚Üí    private val customButtons: MutableMap<Int, Int> = hashMapInit(25) // setup's size
 32‚Üí
 33‚Üí    fun setup(preferences: SharedPreferences) {
 34‚Üí        // NOTE: the default values below should be in sync with preferences_custom_buttons.xml and reviewer.xml
 35‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_undo, "customButtonUndo", SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS)
 36‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_redo, "customButtonRedo", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 37‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_schedule, "customButtonScheduleCard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 38‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_flag, "customButtonFlag", SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS)
 39‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_tag, "customButtonTags", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 40‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_edit, "customButtonEditCard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 41‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_add_note_reviewer, "customButtonAddCard", MENU_DISABLED)
 42‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_replay, "customButtonReplay", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 43‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_card_info, "customButtonCardInfo", MENU_DISABLED)
 44‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_clear_whiteboard, "customButtonClearWhiteboard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 45‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_hide_whiteboard, "customButtonShowHideWhiteboard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS)
 46‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_select_tts, "customButtonSelectTts", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 47‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_open_deck_options, "customButtonDeckOptions", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 48‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_bury, "customButtonBury", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 49‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_bury_card, "customButtonBury", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 50‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_suspend, "customButtonSuspend", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 51‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_suspend_card, "customButtonSuspend", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 52‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_mark_card, "customButtonMarkCard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 53‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_delete, "customButtonDelete", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 54‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_toggle_mic_tool_bar, "customButtonToggleMicToolBar", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 55‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_toggle_whiteboard, "customButtonEnableWhiteboard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 56‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_toggle_stylus, "customButtonToggleStylus", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 57‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_save_whiteboard, "customButtonSaveWhiteboard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 58‚Üí        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_change_whiteboard_pen_color, "customButtonWhiteboardPenColor", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 59‚Üí    }
 60‚Üí
 61‚Üí    private fun setupButton(preferences: SharedPreferences, @IdRes resourceId: Int, preferenceName: String, showAsActionType: Int) {
 62‚Üí        customButtons[resourceId] = preferences.getString(
 63‚Üí            preferenceName,
 64‚Üí            showAsActionType.toString()
 65‚Üí        )!!.toInt()
 66‚Üí    }
 67‚Üí
 68‚Üí    fun setCustomButtons(menu: Menu) {
 69‚Üí        for ((itemId, value) in customButtons) {
 70‚Üí            if (value != MENU_DISABLED) {
 71‚Üí                val item = menu.findItem(itemId)
 72‚Üí                item.setShowAsAction(value)
 73‚Üí            } else {
 74‚Üí                menu.findItem(itemId).isVisible = false
 75‚Üí            }
 76‚Üí        }
 77‚Üí    }
 78‚Üí
 79‚Üí    fun hideWhiteboardIsDisabled(): Boolean {
 80‚Üí        return customButtons[R.id.action_hide_whiteboard] == MENU_DISABLED
 81‚Üí    }
 82‚Üí
 83‚Üí    fun toggleStylusIsDisabled(): Boolean {
 84‚Üí        return customButtons[R.id.action_toggle_stylus] == MENU_DISABLED
 85‚Üí    }
 86‚Üí
 87‚Üí    fun clearWhiteboardIsDisabled(): Boolean {
 88‚Üí        return customButtons[R.id.action_clear_whiteboard] == MENU_DISABLED
 89‚Üí    }
 90‚Üí
 91‚Üí    fun selectTtsIsDisabled(): Boolean {
 92‚Üí        return customButtons[R.id.action_select_tts] == MENU_DISABLED
 93‚Üí    }
 94‚Üí
 95‚Üí    fun saveWhiteboardIsDisabled(): Boolean {
 96‚Üí        return customButtons[R.id.action_save_whiteboard] == MENU_DISABLED
 97‚Üí    }
 98‚Üí
 99‚Üí    fun whiteboardPenColorIsDisabled(): Boolean {
100‚Üí        return customButtons[R.id.action_change_whiteboard_pen_color] == MENU_DISABLED
101‚Üí    }
102‚Üí
103‚Üí    fun suspendIsDisabled(): Boolean = customButtons[R.id.action_suspend] == MENU_DISABLED
104‚Üí
105‚Üí    fun buryIsDisabled(): Boolean = customButtons[R.id.action_bury] == MENU_DISABLED
106‚Üí
107‚Üí    companion object {
108‚Üí        const val SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER = MenuItem.SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER
109‚Üí        const val SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM = MenuItem.SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM
110‚Üí        const val SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS = MenuItem.SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS
111‚Üí        const val MENU_DISABLED = 3
112‚Üí    }
113‚Üí}

 32‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.ankiJsErrorCodeMarkCard
 33‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.ankiJsErrorCodeSetDue
 34‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.ankiJsErrorCodeSuspendCard
